title: Bovine headpiece and method
abstract: There is disclosed a light-based method of causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production, the method comprising the step of shining blue light from an artificial blue light source into just one eye of a bovine, causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production in the bovine, sufficient to induce physiological change. There is further disclosed a device for causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production, the device comprising a headpiece fittable to a bovine's head, and including an artificial blue light source, the headpiece operable to shine blue light from the artificial blue light source into an eye of the bovine, causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production in the bovine, sufficient to induce physiological change.
claims:
Claims 1. A light-based method of causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production, the method comprising the step of shining blue light from an artificial blue light source into just one eye of a bovine, causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production in the bovine, sufficient to induce physiological change. 2. Method of Claim 1, in which the physiological change is a non- therapeutic physiological change. 3. Method of any previous Claim, the method comprising directing blue light into the bovine's eye for at least one selected period of time. 4. Method of any previous Claim, the method including the step of providing to the bovine a combined total of natural and blue light from the artificial blue light source for at least approximately 16 hours during each 24 hour period. 5. Method of any previous Claim, the method including the step of keeping the bovine outdoors whilst the blue light from the artificial blue light source is shone into the just one eye of the bovine. 6. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial light source produces only low intensity blue light. 7. Method of any previous Claim, in which the blue light has a peak wavelength of from 440 to 490 nm. 8. Method of Claim 7, in which the blue light has a peak wavelength of from 459 to 484 nm. 9. Method of Claim 8, in which the blue light has a peak wavelength of 465 nm. 10. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial blue light source includes a white light source. 11. Method of Claim 10, in which the white light source provides blue-enriched light eg. white light from LEDs that have a high blue component. 12. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial blue light source includes a white light LED and a blue filter. 13. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial blue light source comprises at least one blue LED. 14. Method of any of Claims 1 to 11, in which the artificial blue light source includes a white light LED and no blue filter. 15. Method of any of Claims 1 to 13, in which light incident on the just one eye of the bovine is blue. 16. Method of any previous Claim, including the step of directing diffused blue light into the bovine's eye. 17. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial light source provides a blue light intensity of from 10 to 400 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 18. Method of Claim 17, in which the artificial light source provides a blue light intensity of from 100 to 300 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 19. Method of Claim 18, in which the artificial light source provides a blue light intensity of from 200 to 250 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 20. Method of any of Claims 17 to 19, in which the lux levels specified refer to the combined effect of all artificial light sources which are illuminating the bovine's eye. 21. Method of any previous Claim, in which the artificial blue light source comprises more than one artificial light source (e.g. LEDs). 22. Method of any previous Claim, in which the blue light is directed into only a single one of the bovine's eyes at any given time. 23. Method of any previous Claim, including the step of shielding the light source to substantially avoid direct illumination of the bovine's eye. 24. Method of any previous Claim, in which the bovine is a cow, a bull, a heifer, a steer, a calf, an ox, or a buffalo. 25. Method of any previous Claim, wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased milk yield from the cow. 26. Method of Claim 25, wherein the cow is a multiparous cow. 27. Method of Claim 26, wherein the increased milk yield is from 1% to 9%, or from l% to 11%. 28. Method of Claim 25, wherein the cow is a primiparous cow at first lactation. 29. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes one or more of faster onset of puberty, earlier first conception, or improved ovarian growth. 30. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the physiological change includes an increased rise in circulating gonadotropins. 31. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased frequency of estrus behavior, or improved mammary development. 32. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the physiological change includes increased body weight at puberty, or improved feed efficiency, or greater body growth and total weight gain, or increased heart girth, or increased average daily gain (ADG). 33. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the bovine is a heifer, and the physiological change includes increased growth of a pre-pubertal heifer with an initial low body weight, or a heavier heifer at parturition, or a taller heifer at parturition. 34. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the physiological change includes increased carcass protein content, or reduction in carcass subcutaneous fat. 35. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes faster return to reproductive competence post-partum, or increased conception rates and reduced artificial inseminations per animal, or reduction in calving interval, or increases in 60-day non-return rate, or younger first calvers, or reduced risk of twin pregnancies. 36. Method of any of Claims 1 to 24, wherein the bovine is a bull, and the physiological change includes slowed decline in semen viability and concentration as a result of high temperature and humidity, or improved semen quality. 37. A device for causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production, the device comprising a headpiece fittable to a bovine's head, and including an artificial blue light source, the headpiece operable to shine blue light from the artificial blue light source into an eye of the bovine, causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production in the bovine, sufficient to induce physiological change. 38. The device of Claim 37, wherein the headpiece is operable to shine blue light from the artificial blue light source into just one eye of the bovine. 39. The device of Claims 37 or 38, wherein the device is configured to direct blue light into the bovine's eye for at least one selected period of time. 40. The device of any of Claims 37 to 39, wherein the device is configured to provide to the bovine a combined total of natural and blue light from the artificial blue light source for at least approximately 16 hours during each 24 hour period. 41. The device of any of Claims 37 to 40, wherein the device is one in which the artificial light source produces only low intensity blue light. 42. The device of any of Claims 37 to 41, wherein the blue light has a peak wavelength of from 440 to 490 nm. 43. The device of Claim 42, wherein the blue light has a peak wavelength of from 459 to 484 nm. 44. The device of Claim 43, wherein the blue light has a peak wavelength of 465 nm. 45. The device of any of Claims 37 to 44, wherein the artificial blue light source includes a white light source. 46. The device of Claim 45, in which the white light source provides blue-enriched light eg. white light from LEDs that have a high blue component. 47. The device of any of Claims 37 to 46, wherein the artificial blue light source includes a white light LED and a blue filter. 48. The device of any of Claims 37 to 47, wherein the artificial blue light source comprises at least one blue LED. 49. The device of any of Claims 37 to 46, wherein the artificial blue light source includes a white light LED and no blue filter. 50. The device of any of Claims 37 to 48, wherein the device is configured such that light incident on the just one eye of the bovine is blue. 51. The device of any of Claims 37 to 50, wherein the device includes a diffuser arranged to diffuse blue light, wherein the diffused blue light is directed into the bovine's eye. 52. The device of any of Claims 37 to 51, wherein the artificial light source provides 

 a blue light intensity of from 10 to 400 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 53. The device of Claim 52, wherein the artificial light source provides a blue light intensity of from 100 to 300 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 54. The device of Claim 53, wherein the artificial light source provides a blue light intensity of from 200 to 250 lux at the just one eye of the bovine. 55. The device of any of Claims 52 to 54, wherein the lux levels specified refer to the combined effect of all artificial light sources which illuminate the bovine's eye. 56. The device of any of Claims 37 to 55, wherein the artificial blue light source comprises more than one artificial light source (e.g. LEDs). 57. The device of any of Claims 37 to 56, wherein the device is arranged such that the blue light is directed into only a single one of the bovine's eyes at any given time. 58. The device of any of Claims 37 to 57, wherein the light source is shielded to substantially avoid direct illumination of the bovine's eye. 59. The device of any of Claims 37 to 58, wherein the bovine is a cow, a bull, a heifer, a steer, a calf, an ox, or a buffalo. 60. The device of any of Claims 37 to 59, wherein the headpiece comprises a mask. 61. The device of any of Claims 37 to 60, wherein the device includes a control that is capable of turning on the device such that when turned on, the control leaves the device on so that the bovine receives a combined total of natural and blue light from the artificial blue light source for approximately 16 hours during each 24 hour period. 62. The device of any of Claims 37 to 61, wherein the device includes a blinker that covers the eye into which blue light from the artificial blue light source is shone. 63. The device of any of Claims 37 to 62, wherein the device is weatherproof. 64. The device of any of Claims 37 to 63, wherein the device is waterproof. 65. The device of any of Claims 37 to 64, wherein the headpiece contains only a single light source for directing blue light into only a single eye of the bovine. 66. The device of any of Claims 37 to 65, wherein the headpiece includes an automatic timer for turning the light source on and off at selected times. 67. The device of any of Claims 37 to 66, wherein the headpiece comprises a fabric base, the fabric base including eye openings and fasteners for fastening the headpiece around the bovine's head. 68. The device of any of Claims 37 to 67, wherein the headpiece includes a power supply for the light source. 69. The device of Claim 68, including a battery to power the power supply, and further including solar cell apparatus to charge the battery using solar power, or further including a kinetic energy charger apparatus to charge the battery e.g. to charge the battery using movement of the bovine. 70. Use of the device of any of Claims 37 to 69, (i) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased milk yield from the cow; or (it) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes one or more of faster onset of puberty, earlier first conception, or improved ovarian growth; or (iii) the physiological change includes an increased rise in circulating gonadotropins; or (iv) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased frequency of estrus behavior, or improved mammary development; or (v) the physiological change includes increased body weights at puberty, or improved feed efficiency, or greater body growth and total weight gain, or increased heart girth, or increased average daily gain (ADG); or (vi) wherein the bovine is a heifer, and the physiological change includes increases in growth of a pre-pubertal heifer with an initial low body weight, or a heavier heifer at parturition, or a taller heifer at parturition; or 

 (vii) the physiological change includes increased carcass protein content, or reduction in carcass subcutaneous fat; or (viii) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes faster return to reproductive competence post-partum, or increased conception rates and reduced artificial inseminations per animal, or reduction in calving interval, or increases in 60-day non-return rate, or younger first calvers, or reduced risk of twin pregnancies; or (ix) wherein the bovine is a bull, and the physiological change includes slowed decline in semen viability and concentration as a result of high temperature and humidity, or improved semen quality. 71. A system including a device for causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production, the device comprising a headpiece fittable to a bovine's head, and including an artificial blue light source, the headpiece operable to shine blue light from the artificial blue light source into an eye of the bovine, causing a substantial suppression of melatonin production sufficient to induce physiological change, in which the light source is operable to provide blue light at the eye of the bovine, the system including a control in connection with the device, in which the control is configured to turn on the device, wherein the device is configured to direct blue light into the bovine's eye for at least one selected period of time. 72. System of Claim 71, wherein the device is configured to provide the bovine with a combined total of natural and blue light from the artificial blue light source for approximately 16 hours during each 24 hour period. 73. System of Claims 71 or 72, wherein the headpiece is operable to shine blue light from the artificial blue light source into just one eye of the bovine, in which the light source is operable to provide blue light at the just one eye of the bovine. 74. System of any of Claims 71 to 73, wherein the connection is a wired connection. 75. System of any of Claims 71 to 73, wherein the connection is a wireless connection. 76. System of any of Claims 71 to 75, wherein the device is a device of any of Claims 

 37 to 68. 77. Use of the system of any of Claims 71 to 76, (i) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased milk yield from the cow; or (it) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes one or more of faster onset of puberty, earlier first conception, or improved ovarian growth; or (iii) the physiological change includes an increased rise in circulating gonadotropins; or (iv) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes increased frequency of estrus behavior, or improved mammary development; or (v) the physiological change includes increased body weights at puberty, or improved feed efficiency, or greater body growth and total weight gain, or increased heart girth, or increased average daily gain (ADG); or (vi) wherein the bovine is a heifer, and the physiological change includes increases in growth of a pre-pubertal heifer with an initial low body weight, or a heavier heifer at parturition, or a taller heifer at parturition; or (vii) the physiological change includes increased carcass protein content, or reduction in carcass subcutaneous fat; or (viii) wherein the bovine is a cow, and the physiological change includes faster return to reproductive competence post-partum, or increased conception rates and reduced artificial inseminations per animal, or reduction in calving interval, or increases in 60-day non-return rate, or younger first calvers, or reduced risk of twin pregnancies; or (ix) wherein the bovine is a bull, and the physiological change includes slowed decline in semen viability and concentration as a result of high temperature and humidity, or improved semen quality.
